content.problemStatement:
You are given a 0−indexed0-indexed0−indexed integer array numsnumsnums. Your task is to find the leftmost equilibrium index in the array. An equilibrium index is defined as an index iii such that the sum of the elements to the left of iii is equal to the sum of the elements to the right of iii.

Formally, the equilibrium index iii satisfies the condition:

sum(nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i−1])=sum(nums[i+1]+nums[i+2]+...+nums[nums.length−1])sum(nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i−1])=sum(nums[i+1]+nums[i+2]+...+nums[nums.length−1])sum(nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i−1])=sum(nums[i+1]+nums[i+2]+...+nums[nums.length−1])

**Conditions**:


If iii is 000, the left sum is considered 000.
If iii is (n−1)(n - 1)(n−1), the right sum is considered 000.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line contains an integer NNN, length of array.
The next line contain an array numsnumsnums of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, return the leftmost equilibrium index if it exists, or return −1-1−1 if there is no such index.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the leftmost equilibrium index, or -1 if no such index exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: index 0 has left sum 0 and right sum (-1+2+1-2)=0, so answer 0.\nTest case 2: index 2 has left sum (1+(-1))=0 and right sum 0, so answer 2.\nTest case 3: no index satisfies the condition, output -1.
With a single element, both left and right sums are 0, so index 0 is an equilibrium.
No index yields equal left and right sums; therefore output is -1.

solution.approach: Compute the total sum of the array. Then iterate from left to right keeping a running left sum. For each index i, the right sum equals total - left - nums[i]. If left equals right, i is an equilibrium index. Because we scan from left to right, the first such i is the leftmost. This runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space (apart from storing the array).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the input array (can be O(1) if processed on the fly).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        total = sum(arr)
        left = 0
        ans = -1
        for i, val in enumerate(arr):
            right = total - left - val
            if left == right:
                ans = i
                break
            left += val
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
